name: Hoist 128x128 Hot-Band Shared Offsets Out Of The Steady-State Loop

summary.name: Hoist 128x128 Hot-Band Shared Offsets Out Of The Steady-State Loop
summary.hypothesis: Both active 128x128 hot-band kernels still carry invariant warp-local shared offset arithmetic through the K loop even though row and column warp positions are fixed for the CTA lifetime. Hoisting those offsets once per warp should shave integer address work and reduce control dilution on a surface that is already close to the branch best.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local shared-pointer arithmetic and hot-loop control overhead are small but still relevant on the accepted compact PTX base.
summary.risk: Low. This is a bounded control-overhead cleanup and should not change tile geometry or shared-memory budgeting.
ranking_notes: This is the cleanest low-risk exploit path once the compact PTX base is already close to the best branch result.
It attacks loop-carried overhead without touching synchronization semantics or tiling.
It ranks below dir_01 because barrier is still a larger measured tax than pointer arithmetic.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the hoist materially grows registers or requires per-iteration predicates to be replaced with larger control scaffolding.
expected_local_changes: Hoist warp-local A and B shared offsets once per warp before the K loop in the active 128x128 hot-band kernels.
Leave synchronization, grouped_rows, and tiling unchanged.
Keep the change limited to loop-invariant address setup.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
